data.title: Kaspersky: Cheap Android phones are available with cryptocurrency-stealing malware pre-installed
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News Cybersecurity company Kaspersky reported in a recent analysis that Triada Trojan virus has evolved into one of the most complex and dangerous Android threats because it is able to infiltrate every process on a smartphone. In the latest version, hackers have deeply implanted malware into the system framework of knockoff smartphones, making it extremely difficult to detect and delete. Some cheap Android phones currently on sale are pre-installed with malware that specifically attacks unsuspecting Android users-stealing cryptocurrencies, replacing phone numbers during calls, and hijacking their social media accounts.
The report said that between March 13 and 27, 2025, more than 2,600 users encountered the Trojan, which gave attackers "almost unlimited control" over their smartphones. The malware can steal user credentials from messaging apps such as Telegram and TikTok, replace crypto wallet addresses, and even hijack victims' communications by sending messages on their behalf. (Decrypt)

data.title: 80% of Total SHIB Supply Concentrated in Few Hands — What's Impact?
Bybit and Zodia Custody Partner to Enhance Security Services for Institutional Clients
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: The cryptocurrency exchange Bybit was previously hacked and lost $1.45 billion. The exchange announced that it has cooperated with Zodia Custody to enhance its security services for institutional clients. Zodia Custody currently has the support of a number of heavyweight traditional financial (TradFi) companies such as Standard Chartered Bank, providing independent custody and over-the-counter settlement services. Users can trade on Bybit, and their assets will be held by Zodia, thereby reducing exchange risks and preventing funds from being mixed. (CoinDesk)

data.title: Citigroup Analyst Advises Caution on Risk Assets Following Tariff Announcement
data.description: According to BlockBeats, Citigroup analyst Kate Moore has recommended that investors exercise caution and avoid risk assets following U.S. President Donald Trump's announcement of new tariff policies. Moore's advice comes amid concerns about potential market volatility and economic implications stemming from the tariff measures.

data.title: Crypto IRA: Fidelity allows Bitcoin, Ethereum and Litecoin in retirement planning
data.description:
Asset management giant Fidelity offers tax-advantaged crypto IRAs with Bitcoin, Ethereum, and Litecoin, secured in cold wallets. The article Crypto IRA: Fidelity allows Bitcoin, Ethereum and Litecoin in retirement planning was first published on Crypto Valley Journal.
source: https://cryptovalleyjournal.com/investing/financial-products/crypto-ira-fidelity-enables-bitcoin-ethereum-and-litecoin-in-retirement-planning/?utm_campaign=rss_reader&utm_medium=rss&utm_source=feed_source&utm_content=Crypto+IRA%3A+Fidelity+allows+Bitcoin%2C+Ethereum+and+Litecoin+in+retirement+planning

data.title: South Korean Financial Services Commission official: Foreigners may be allowed to invest in crypto assets in South Korea in the future
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: Kim Sung-jin, director of the Virtual Assets Section of the Financial Services Commission of South Korea, said in the National Assembly on April 2 that if the country's crypto exchanges have sufficient anti-money laundering capabilities, it may be possible to open up foreigners to invest in crypto assets in South Korea in the future. Current laws still prohibit foreign legal persons and individuals from participating in such investments. (Bloter)

data.title: U.S. Corporate Layoffs Reach Record High in March
data.description: According to Odaily, the number of corporate layoffs in the United States reached 275,240 in March, marking the highest level since May 2020. The monthly rate of layoffs increased by 60.01%, compared to the previous rate of 245.45%. Additionally, the annual rate of layoffs surged to 204.8%, up from the previous rate of 103.20%.

data.title: Greeks.live: The crypto market sees Trump's reciprocal tariffs as negative news, and the current key price of BTC is $83,000-87,000
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: Greeks.live macro researcher Adam released an English community briefing, pointing out that after Trump announced a 10% comprehensive tariff and a 25% auto tariff, the overall sentiment of the group was bearish. While this was below market expectations, it was still seen as negative news overall. The key level of interest is $83,000-87,000, which was previously a support area, with consensus agreeing that $85,200 was the first liquidation level triggered during the initial rally. Trump’s tariffs are seen as a significant trade disruption, especially those on ships that could increase port calls by $1 million and significantly disrupt global supply chains. The market initially reacted positively, with prices briefly rising to $88,000 in what was seen as short covering, before a sharp reversal as the market realized the economic impact. Options liquidity remains strongly bearish, with traders noting heavy put option buying, including "700 $79,000 puts expiring at the end of April."
